page: Microsoft Azure D128s_v6 is a Dsv6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. The system provides 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache. There are no local storage drives or GPUs bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities alongside average single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls in the average category, whereas the multi-core CPU performance is rated as strong. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks reach top-tier levels. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, achieve top-tier ratings. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests.

This server type is suited for highly parallelized workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, where it demonstrates top-tier throughput. It also supports large language model inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds for large models and strong text generation speeds. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM provides a balanced resource allocation for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for disk-intensive tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v6 is a Dsv6-family virtual machine configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 64 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 260 MB L3 cache. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling strong performance in cryptography and multi-threaded decompression. This server is suited for heavy multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and large language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v6 is a Dsv6 virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v6 is a Dsv6 virtual machine with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-core, memory-balanced virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of system memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated cryptography and matrix math
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and LLM inference
